description_long: This object is a vintage biplane with two sets of parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by struts and wires. The fuselage is slender and tapers towards the tail, featuring a green body with tan wings and tail surfaces. The front of the airplane displays a prominent radial engine with a two-bladed propeller, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in matching tan coloration. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, suitable for display or historical urban settings.
description: A vintage biplane with tan double wings, green fuselage, open cockpit, and a front-mounted radial engine with a two-bladed propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the radial engine, two-bladed propeller, and the stacked biplane wings with visible struts and wires.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the open cockpit, the length of the green fuselage, and the tail skid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tapering fuselage, tail assembly with vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the biplane wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit opening, engine cowling, and the undercarriage with main wheels and tail skid.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
